title: Brain Evals
description: Test cases with known correct outcomes for validating agent decisions. See spec/BRAIN_SPEC.md §03-evals.json for requirements.

service.description: The service this eval set covers. Must match the service definition in 01-service-definition.md.

version.description: Eval set version. Increment when cases change. Format: semver or date string (e.g. '1.0', '2025-05').

evals.description: Test cases. Minimum 10, with at least 3 rated 'hard'.

id.description: Unique identifier for this case. Format: EVAL-001, EVAL-002, etc.

description.description: One sentence describing what this case tests.

difficulty.description: routine: the obvious case. edge: unusual but has a clear rule. hard: non-obvious; a smart new hire would likely get it wrong.

source.description: Where this case came from. Examples: 'historical ticket #1234', 'Priya interview 2025-04', 'maintenance log 2025-03-12'.

input.description: The input the agent receives. Shape must match the relevant skill's input contract in 04-skills.md.

expected_output.description: The correct output. Must be verifiable — not 'agent handles it well' but a specific, checkable result.

decision.description: What the agent should decide or do.

action.description: The specific action the agent should take, if applicable.

escalate.description: Whether the agent should escalate to a human.

escalation_reason.description: Why it escalates, if escalate is true.

notes.description: Anything a reviewer needs to know when checking the agent's output against this case.
